body:
Title: CRA Form RC381 2025 Inter-Provincial Calculation for CPP and QPP Contributions and Overpayments.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/rc381/rc381-25e.pdf

CRA Form RC381 2025 is completed when a taxpayer earned employment income in Quebec and was resident outside Quebec on December 31, 2025, or earned employment income outside Quebec and was resident in Quebec on December 31, 2025. Part 3 calculates CPP and QPP contributions on employment income.

Part 3 line 1 enters CPP pensionable earnings from T4 slips where the province of employment is not Quebec. Line 2 enters QPP pensionable earnings from T4 slips where the province of employment is Quebec. Line 3 adds lines 1 and 2. Line 6 divides line 4 by line 5 to allocate the Part 2 CPP and QPP limits. Lines 7 through 10 multiply CPP Part 2 lines E, C, F, and D by the line 6 allocation rate. Line 11 is the lesser of line 1 and line 7. Line 15 is the lesser of line 10 and the excess of line 11 over line 8. Line 18 subtracts line 15 and line 17 from line 16 and floors the result at zero. Line 20 multiplies actual CPP base and first additional contributions by 83.1933%. Line 21 subtracts line 20 from line 19. Line 22 multiplies line 18 by 4.95% and is capped at $3,356.10. Line 23 multiplies line 18 by 1% and is capped at $678.00. Line 26 multiplies line 15 by 4% and is capped at $396.00.

For QPP employment income, lines 27 through 30 subtract CPP lines 7 through 10 from QPP Part 2 lines I, G, J, and H. Line 31 is the lesser of QPP earnings line 2 and line 27. Line 35 is the lesser of line 30 and the excess of line 31 over line 28. Line 38 subtracts line 35 and line 37 from line 36 and floors the result at zero. Line 40 multiplies actual QPP base and first additional contributions by 84.375%. Line 41 subtracts line 40 from line 39. Line 42 multiplies line 38 by 5.4% and is capped at $3,661.20. Line 43 multiplies line 38 by 1% and is capped at $678.00. Line 46 multiplies line 35 by 4% and is capped at $396.00.

Lines 49, 52, 53, 56, 59, 60, 61, 64, 67, 68, and 69 total actual and required CPP/QPP base, first additional, and second additional contributions and calculate the overall difference. If line 69 is positive, Part 3a line 70 enters line 52 on line 30800 of the return, line 73 adds lines 71 and 72 and enters the result on line 22215, and line 74 enters line 69 as an overpayment on line 44800.

Numeric grounding: RC381 line 20 rate is 83.1933%, normalized as 0.831933. RC381 line 40 rate is 84.375%, normalized as 0.84375. CPP rates are 4.95%, 1%, and 4%, normalized as 0.0495, 0.01, and 0.04. QPP rates are 5.4%, 1%, and 4%, normalized as 0.054, 0.01, and 0.04. CPP caps are $3,356.10, $678.00, and $396.00. QPP caps are $3,661.20, $678.00, and $396.00.
citation_label: CRA Form RC381 2025 Inter-Provincial CPP/QPP Employment Contributions
heading: CRA Form RC381 2025 Inter-Provincial Calculation for CPP and QPP Contributions and Overpayments
